business_angle: Brian McGuinness is the Coatbridge and east-Glasgow decorator trusted with period and heritage homes - Victorian cornices and ceilings, tenement and period flats, halls and staircases over several floors - painting and wallpaper done to a high standard, clean and tidy, by the same hands every time.
why_this_trader_wins: The reviews repeatedly describe the same specialism that most general decorators avoid: Jim's flat was painted to a very high standard with particular praise for the Victorian cornices and ceilings; Bob's job was a hall across three floors plus wallpapering a bedroom; Helena's was a whole house including the hall and staircase; Linda's was painting and papering across three rooms. This is careful period-property decorating - intact original plasterwork, multi-floor hall/stair/landing runs, and paper-hanging - not quick repaints. It is backed by an unusually strong 5.0 on Google from 98 reviews and the recurring words reliable, trustworthy, neat and tidy.
target_customer: Coatbridge, North Lanarkshire and east-Glasgow homeowners (Airdrie, Bargeddie, Bellshill, Coatbridge, the Glasgow east end and the surrounding ML and east-end postcodes) who own a period or tenement home and want the cornices, ceilings, halls and staircases painted and papered properly, by a decorator they can trust to be clean, tidy and reliable.
page_single_job: Get that homeowner to request a quote (or call) for a painting, wallpapering or period-decorating job.

customer_praise_themes.theme: period plasterwork: Victorian cornices and ceilings done well
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[4] (Jim): 'painted all the rooms in our flat to a very high standard. Especially pleased by his good work on the Victorian cornices and ceilings'

customer_praise_themes.theme: multi-floor hall, staircase and landing runs
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[3] (Bob): 'paint our hall (3 floors), bathroom and wallpaper our bedroom'; reviews[1] (Helena): 'painting my house to include the hall and staircase'; reviews[2] (Jodie): 'living room and Hallway'
brief.json reviews[0] (Linda): 'the quality and finish of his painting and papering superb'; reviews[3] (Bob): 'wallpaper our bedroom'
brief.json reviews[0] (Linda): 'extremely neat and very tidy'; reviews[1] (Helena): 'spotlessly clean worker'

customer_praise_themes.theme: reliable and trustworthy, finished to the timescale agreed
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[4] (Jim): 'a reliable and trustworthy decorator'; reviews[1] (Helena): 'very reliable'; reviews[0] (Linda): 'completing the job in timescale given'; reviews[3] (Bob): 'the job was completed in the agreed time'

customer_praise_themes.theme: puts a problem right quickly, repeat custom
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Jodie): 'a small area had dried patchy. One message to Brian and he was out within the hour to sort it ... will use them again'; reviews[0] (Linda): 'I shall without doubt be back in touch again'

named_people.name: Brian
named_people.basis: business_name is 'Brian McGuinness Decorators' (brief.json business_name) and Brian is named by name in all 5 of the strongest reviews (Linda, Helena, Jodie, Bob, Jim). This overrides brief.json contact_name_usage_allowed=false, which fired on a FALSE extraction - the possible_contact_name 'Outstanding' is the first word of Linda's review ('Outstanding Decorator...'), not a real name. The actual proprietor name 'Brian' is in the business name itself and densely review-evidenced. Same judgment-call basis as the Kyle Knowles / Damo ARCH-2 precedent.

named_people.name: Chris
named_people.basis: brief.json reviews[2] (Jodie): 'Brian and Chris done an amazing job'. Chris is named once, as someone Brian works with. Usable as 'Brian and Chris' / 'Brian, sometimes with Chris' but not as a co-owner or to invent a team size. Keep it light and factual.

strongest_review_quote.text: Brian has painted all the rooms in our flat to a very high standard. Especially pleased by his good work on the Victorian cornices and ceilings. All Brian's work has been excellent, a reliable and trustworthy decorator. We would happily recommend him!
strongest_review_quote.author: Jim

real_services.name: Period and heritage decorating
real_services.evidence: reviews[4] Jim (Victorian cornices and ceilings, flat, very high standard); photos[] period rooms with ornate cornice/coving and ceiling roses

real_services.name: Interior painting
real_services.evidence: reviews[1] Helena (whole house: hall, staircase, living/dining room, kitchen); reviews[0] Linda (3 rooms); reviews[2] Jodie (living room and hallway); photos[] finished painted rooms

real_services.name: Wallpaper and paper-hanging
real_services.evidence: reviews[0] Linda ('painting and papering superb'); reviews[3] Bob ('wallpaper our bedroom'); photos[] floral feature wall + botanical cloakroom paper

real_services.name: Halls, stairs and landings
real_services.evidence: reviews[3] Bob ('hall (3 floors)'); reviews[1] Helena ('hall and staircase'); reviews[2] Jodie ('Hallway'); photos[] stairwell with feature wall and spindle banister

claims_to_avoid: any certification or membership (Dulux Select, PDA, Checkatrade Vetted, TrustATrader, Gas Safe, NICEIC) - none verified; directory_probes show 0 verified profiles (Yell/MyBuilder/Trustpilot are snippet-only, exact-name single-signal; TrustATrader was an identity homonym and rejected; Checkatrade/RatedPeople/Bark/Houzz/MyJobQuote NOT_FOUND)
the proprietor's surname-led personal backstory or an owner monologue - there is no self-description from Brian in evidence
the street address '2 Seamill Way' anywhere public - use Coatbridge / ML5 only

'Bathroom installations' as a service: brief.json services_meta lists it but the only source is a MISREAD of Bob's review, where 'bathroom' is one of the rooms Brian PAINTED ('paint our hall (3 floors), bathroom and wallpaper our bedroom'). Brian paints bathrooms; he does not fit/install bathrooms. Do not list bathroom installation as a service.
'painters and decorators' / 'Painting and decorating' duplicated as separate services (brief.json services[] has near-duplicates and a niche label) - they are one trade, not multiple services

claiming Glasgow city centre as the base or a city-centre address - the registered base is Coatbridge ML5 (North Lanarkshire), about 10mi east of Glasgow city centre; frame coverage honestly as Coatbridge, North Lanarkshire and the east of Glasgow

service areas beyond a plausible one-van radius of Coatbridge ML5 - Coatbridge is the evidenced base; the surrounding North Lanarkshire towns and east-Glasgow districts are UK-geographic derivations from ML5 and must stay plausible (no west-Glasgow or national footprint)
naming Chris as a co-owner, or inventing a team/crew size - Chris is named once in one review as someone Brian works with
personality: calm, traditional, dependable; a craftsman who treats an old house's plasterwork with care, keeps the place clean while he works, and turns up when he says he will
evidence_strength: moderate-content / single-source (rich, specific Google reviews that name the heritage specialism directly + 10 completed-project photos; no third-party corroboration - no Facebook, no verified directory profile)
pitch_hook_summary: Lead on the period-home angle (Victorian cornices and ceilings, tenement flats, halls and staircases over several floors, painting and wallpaper, clean and tidy), backed by a perfect 5.0 on Google from 98 reviews.

metadata.source_quality_reason: Source enrichment not complete; no verified third-party profile; contactability NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW (no email, UK mobile only)
metadata.manual_review_note: Single-source (Google-only) with a LOCATION_MISMATCH_NEEDS_REVIEW flag (Google city Coatbridge differs from the prospect region Glasgow). Resolved for build purposes by framing coverage HONESTLY around the real Coatbridge ML5 base in North Lanarkshire / east Glasgow, never claiming Glasgow city centre. This is a BUILD run; pitch_gate enforces at outreach time, not build time. The evidence is specific and usable (same posture as the Kyle Knowles reference build, which was also source_quality=FAIL with rich Google-only evidence).
metadata.authored_blind: Path B authored from frozen evidence + CLAUDE.md + the webfortrades-site-design skill only. Kyle's palette (wet-slate ink #1B2420 + viridian-teal #0F6E5C; Bricolage Grotesque + Instrument Sans) and Damo's palette (warm-iron #22201C + cedar-amber #A96B23; Oswald + Hanken Grotesk) are off-limits, as are all library pairings and the three AI clusters. This build uses an independent heritage-tenement palette (muted sage-green + chalk lime-white + aged pewter) with Newsreader (period editorial serif) + Mulish (quiet humanist sans), grounded in the period-home subject. Two other painter sites build in parallel (boutique aubergine+brass; bright white+eucalyptus+oak); this one stays anchored to the muted heritage-sage seed so all three differ.
